title: Planetary Boundaries
focus: Concept of Planetary Boundaries, the 9 specific processes, and their current transgression status according to the Planetary Health Check 2025.
rationale: This is a distinct scientific concept regarding ecological thresholds, separate from the organizational details of UNEP.
sectionExcerpt: The Planetary Health Check (PHC) 2025, released by the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research (PIK), provides a scientific assessment regarding Planetary Boundaries (PBs). PBs are ecological thresholds defining the safe limits... 7 of the 9 PBs have been transgressed except Atmospheric Aerosol Loading & Stratospheric Ozone Depletion and Ocean Acidification crossed its safe operating space for the first time.
evidenceBullets: Planetary Health Check 2025 released by Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research (PIK).
Planetary Boundaries are 9 ecological thresholds defining humanity's safe operating space.
Atmospheric Aerosol Loading and Stratospheric Ozone Depletion have not been transgressed.
Ocean Acidification crossed its safe operating space for the first time.

title: United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
focus: UNEP's genesis, funding, hosted entities (IPCC, IPBES), administered multilateral agreements, and key reports.
rationale: UNEP is a major international organization with extensive factual details (conventions, reports) that form a distinct, highly examinable cluster.
sectionExcerpt: Location: Nairobi, Kenya. Genesis: 1972, following UN Conference on Human Environment... Hosts the secretariats of various conventions and Entities: IPBES, IPCC... Major Global Multilateral Agreements under UNEP: CITES, CMS, Vienna, Basel, CBD, Rotterdam, Stockholm, Minamata... Reports: Emission Gap, Adaptation Gap, Global Environment Outlook 7.
evidenceBullets: Established in 1972 following the UN Conference on Human Environment, headquartered in Nairobi, Kenya.
Relies on voluntary contributions for over 95% of funding; Environment Fund is its core fund.
Hosts secretariats for IPBES and IPCC (jointly with WMO).
Administers major agreements including CITES, CMS, Vienna Convention, Basel, CBD, Rotterdam, Stockholm, and Minamata Conventions.
Publishes key reports: Emission Gap Report, Adaptation Gap Report, Global Environment Outlook, and State of Finance for Forests.
Supported the Common Carbon Metric for assessing building operations' carbon footprint.
